The city grade separation com.
mission decided yesterday to in
vlte d. c. Corbin to conference
as soon as he returns to the city.
This will probably be Friday or
Saturday. Jay p. Graves was at
the meeting, but nothing was given
out as to his views on the sub
ject of common right of way, tun-
mel through Cannon hill and union
depot, as proposed in City Engineer
Ralston's scheme.


The belief is that Graves was
not ut the meeting as "A citizen or
a representative Of the streetcar
lines. but as representative of
those interested with him In his
tunnel along Front av. Mayor
Moore said today that with such a
commission rs he has gathered
something tangible should develop
In reasonable time.


The council last night decided to
hold another grade separation
beeting next Monday night.
